process.name: Residential New Single-Family Residence Permit Workflow

stages.name: Research property and project scope
stages.description: Confirm the site, proposed residential work, applicable property constraints, and whether the project is a new single-family residence before preparing an application package.

evidence.note: Public PP&D residential permit guidance identifies new single-family residence permits as a residential building permit workflow.

stages.name: Prepare application, plans, calculations, and supporting documents
stages.description: Prepare the application materials, searchable drawing plan PDF, structural or energy calculations when applicable, and supporting documents required for plan review.

evidence.note: Public guidance says projects using the Single PDF Process submit drawing plans as one searchable PDF and applications, calculations, and supporting documents as separate PDFs.

stages.name: Create or use DevHub account
stages.description: Use DevHub with PortlandOregon.gov credentials for online permitting services; automation fixtures only record public workflow requirements and do not create accounts or authenticate.

evidence.note: Public DevHub guidance identifies DevHub as Portland's online permitting system and says PortlandOregon.gov credentials are used for authenticated services.

stages.name: Select building permit request type
stages.description: Choose the building permit request path for a new single-family residence or detached accessory dwelling unit when that is the matching project type.

evidence.note: Public application guidance lists building permits and new single-family residence or detached ADU permits among DevHub request types.

stages.name: Enter application data
stages.description: Enter applicant, owner, contractor, property, valuation, occupancy, and project-description information needed for review.

evidence.note: Public DevHub application guidance describes permit requests requiring plan review through the online permitting workflow.

stages.name: Upload submittal documents
stages.description: Upload the drawing plan PDF and separate application, calculation, and supporting-document PDFs in the required document groups.

evidence.note: Single PDF Process guidance separates drawing plans from application, calculation, and supporting PDFs.

stages.name: Review acknowledgments and submit request
stages.description: The applicant reviews acknowledgments or certifications and submits the official permit request only after explicit confirmation.

evidence.note: Public DevHub guidance treats online application submission as part of the permit request workflow.

stages.name: Prescreen and intake
stages.description: PP&D reviews the submitted package for intake completeness before routing to plan review or requesting corrections.

evidence.note: Public DevHub FAQ identifies permit requests requiring plan review and fee payment services.

stages.name: Pay required fees
stages.description: Pay intake, review, issuance, or other required fees only after the user has explicitly authorized the payment action for the session.

evidence.note: Public DevHub FAQ lists fee payments as an online permitting service category.

stages.name: Plan review
stages.description: PP&D plan reviewers evaluate the residential building permit submittal; unresolved review comments may produce checksheets or correction requests.

evidence.note: Public DevHub FAQ lists correction uploads as part of online permit services.

stages.name: Respond to corrections
stages.description: Prepare correction responses and revised PDFs, then upload official correction materials only with explicit user confirmation.

evidence.note: Public DevHub FAQ identifies correction upload services for online permit workflows.

stages.name: Approval and issuance
stages.description: After required reviews and fees are resolved, the permit may be approved and issued for work to proceed under permit conditions.

evidence.note: Residential building permit guidance describes PP&D review before permit issuance.

stages.name: Inspections
stages.description: Schedule and complete required inspections after issuance; automation must stop before scheduling unless the user gives exact confirmation.

evidence.note: Public DevHub FAQ lists inspection scheduling for online trade permits; this residential fixture treats scheduling as consequential and user-confirmed when available.

stages.name: Closeout, expiration, extension, or reactivation
stages.description: Track final approval, closeout status, and any expiration, extension, or reactivation path that may apply to the issued residential permit.

evidence.note: Permit lifecycle tracking belongs to the residential permit workflow after issuance and inspections.

requiredFacts.label: Property address or property identifier
requiredFacts.description: The permit request needs the property location or identifier for the proposed residence.
requiredFacts.validationHint: Ask for the site address or public property identifier; do not store private owner contact values in committed fixtures.

evidence.note: Residential permit guidance is property-specific and requires site identification.

requiredFacts.label: Project description and scope
requiredFacts.description: Describe the proposed new residence, detached ADU relationship when applicable, and major construction scope.
requiredFacts.validationHint: Ask for a concise scope summary sufficient to route the request and document checklist.

evidence.note: DevHub application guidance routes request types based on project type.

requiredFacts.label: Applicant contact information
requiredFacts.description: Applicant identity and contact details are required in the live application, but committed fixtures must keep values redacted or absent.
requiredFacts.validationHint: Ask the user for the value during a real case; do not include the value in fixture JSON.

evidence.note: Online application data entry requires applicant information in the authenticated workflow.

requiredFacts.label: Owner information
requiredFacts.description: Owner information is needed for the permit record and authorization context.
requiredFacts.validationHint: Collect from the user only in the case workspace, not in committed fixtures.

evidence.note: Residential building permit applications require project-party information.

requiredFacts.label: Contractor license information when a contractor performs the work
requiredFacts.description: Contractor license details are conditional when the application identifies a contractor rather than owner-performed work.
requiredFacts.validationHint: Ask whether a contractor is involved and collect license details only when applicable.

evidence.note: Residential permit application data can include contractor and license details.

requiredFacts.label: Estimated project valuation
requiredFacts.description: Estimated valuation supports fee assessment and review routing for the building permit request.
requiredFacts.validationHint: Ask for the project valuation or cost basis before fee-related steps.

evidence.note: Fee payment is part of the DevHub service set; valuation is a typical fee input for building permits.

requiredDocuments.name: Building permit application PDF
requiredDocuments.description: Application information must be provided through the online workflow and supporting application PDF where required by the selected permit path.

evidence.note: Single PDF Process guidance keeps applications separate from the drawing plan PDF.

requiredDocuments.name: Single searchable drawing plan PDF
requiredDocuments.description: Drawing sheets for the residence are grouped into one searchable PDF when the Single PDF Process applies.

evidence.note: Public guidance says drawing plans are one searchable PDF in the Single PDF Process.

requiredDocuments.name: Structural calculations PDF when applicable
requiredDocuments.description: Structural calculations are submitted as a separate PDF when the project design requires them.

evidence.note: Single PDF Process guidance treats calculations as separate PDFs from the drawing plan file.

requiredDocuments.name: Energy or code compliance supporting documents
requiredDocuments.description: Energy, code compliance, site, or technical reports are included as separate supporting PDFs when applicable to the new residence.

evidence.note: Single PDF Process guidance separates supporting documents from the drawing plan PDF.

requiredDocuments.name: Correction response and revised plan PDFs
requiredDocuments.description: When reviewers issue corrections, upload response materials and revised PDFs through the correction path after user confirmation.

evidence.note: Public DevHub FAQ identifies correction uploads as an online service category.

fileRules.description: Drawing plans are submitted as one searchable PDF when the Single PDF Process applies.

evidence.note: Single PDF Process guidance requires drawing plans as one searchable PDF.

fileRules.description: Applications, calculations, and supporting documents are separate PDFs rather than bundled into the drawing plan file.

evidence.note: Single PDF Process guidance lists applications, calculations, and supporting documents as separate PDFs.

feePaymentGates.description: Do not attempt payment until a fee notice or payment step is available for the permit request.
feePaymentGates.requiredConfirmation: I confirm I want to pay the listed fees for this residential permit request in this DevHub session.

evidence.note: Public DevHub FAQ lists fee payments as an online service category.

feePaymentGates.description: Issuance or remaining fees are a financial stop point before relying on the permit as issued.
feePaymentGates.requiredConfirmation: I confirm I want to pay the listed issuance or remaining fees for this residential permit request in this DevHub session.

evidence.note: Fee payment is a public DevHub service and is treated as financial.

actionGates.name: Preview reversible draft field entries
actionGates.description: Draft field entry may be previewed with source-backed user facts before any live workflow changes.

evidence.note: Application data entry is part of the online request workflow; this fixture permits only reversible draft previews.

actionGates.name: Official document upload confirmation
actionGates.description: Uploading official application or correction documents must stop for exact user confirmation.
actionGates.requiredConfirmation: I confirm I want to upload these residential permit documents in this DevHub session.

evidence.note: Correction uploads and permit-request document uploads are official workflow actions and are treated as consequential.

actionGates.name: Submit permit application confirmation
actionGates.description: Submitting the permit request is an official action and requires exact user confirmation.
actionGates.requiredConfirmation: I confirm I want to submit this residential permit application in this DevHub session.

evidence.note: Online permit application submission is part of the DevHub permit request workflow.

actionGates.name: Payment confirmation
actionGates.description: Fee payment is financial and must never be automated without exact session-specific confirmation.
actionGates.requiredConfirmation: I confirm I want to pay the listed fees for this residential permit request in this DevHub session.

evidence.note: Public DevHub FAQ lists fee payments; the fixture classifies payment as financial.

actionGates.name: Inspection scheduling confirmation
actionGates.description: Inspection scheduling changes the permit workflow and must stop for exact user confirmation.
actionGates.requiredConfirmation: I confirm I want to schedule the selected inspection for this residential permit in this DevHub session.

evidence.note: Public DevHub FAQ lists inspection scheduling; this fixture treats scheduling as consequential.
